column3:
Lab: This refers to documents related to laboratory tests and results related to blood and these blood test results are meticulously recorded, often accompanied by reference ranges to provide context for interpretation. These documents may also provides information about test results related to the patient's blood sample (Whole blood) which is used to detect the presence of antibodies. This also includes documents generated from Public Health Ontario Laboratory. This also includes diagnostic for iron deficiency, Hepatitis B surface antigen test, test for amount of Urea or Creatinine in blood.

Consult: Consult documents encompass written records or communications exchanged between healthcare professionals, including responses to referrals, interactions with other doctors, ED Consultations, Discharge Summary, sleep apnea diagnosis and treatment plan, pathology conducted by a dermatologist who has seen the patient, and ED Reports. These documents may also serve to seek the expertise or opinion of another colleague to aid in diagnosing, treating, or managing a patient's medical condition, fostering collaboration and knowledge sharing among healthcare providers involved in the patient's care. This may also include detailed explanation of a procedures, along with other relevant details such as Reason for Consultation, Doctor's Notes, Reports, Discussion and Analysis, Treatment Plan, Follow-up Instructions, Conclusion. This may also includes Operative Note or a Consultation Note from a physician or from a dermatologist discussing the patient's condition, the procedure, findings, and post-op diagnosis for a patient. This might include deatils or documents about 'Radiology Report', 'Pathology Report' or 'Diagnostics Report', but its primary purpose is to provide advice or consultancy to a patient. A 'Consult' document may rely upon other documents for its conclusions or recommendations. With 'Consult' documents always consider the context in which the document is used for and the primary purpose of the document. This documents will have the phrase 'The patient was seen in consultation regarding:'

Insurance: This documents contains request for health information health insurance companies (such as WSIB, Sun Life, Canada Life, Manulife, Dynacare, Desjardin, RBC Insurance, IA Financial, SSQ) for the purpose of assessing a health insurance claim. These documents request for information related to a person’s medical condition, disability, accident or injury. This refers to documents related to patient insurance information, including request for medical records such as specific clinical notes, consultation reports, and test results, for the purpose of assessing a disability or health insurance claim, policy details, coverage, claims, and billing.

Legal: This encompasses documents related to the request and release of medical records by a law firm or lawyer acting on behalf of a client with proper authorization or if the document has information stating that the law firm represent the individual mentioned in the document with regards to the 
motor vehicle accident benefits. It includes signed authorization forms from the client, specific requests for the release of medical records, legal correspondence, or documentation from the law firm or lawyer indicating representation. The request includes requesting All Clinical Notes and Records, Operative Reports, Pathology Reports, Consultation Notes, Physician Notes, Treatment Records, Laboratory Results, Emergency Room Records, Discharge Summaries, Diagnostic Imaging Reports, Electrocardiograms (ECGs), Product Identification Labels, and Billing History/Statement of Accounts. Documents in this category also include correspondence letters or notes between lawyers and doctors, between law firms and medical clinics.

Old Chart: This refers to documents that includes patients previous medical reports or records, patients previous consulation records, patients previous lab reports and patients medical history with all the past medical records in the same document. These documents will also have information about patients medical history dating months or years along with patients ongoing medical concerns. If the provided EMR document was generated or received months or years ago compared to the current date, it is categorically considered as an 'Old Chart'. If the document contains two or more documents about patients medical history. always look for the EMR document sampling time, receiving time, report time, generated dates, received dates, recorded dates and if these dates are years old when comparing with the current it is always an 'Old Chart'.

Radiology: This refers to radiology reports from a radiologist. These report provides detailed findings from the imaging studies such as X-rays, MRIs, CT scans, DXA scan, Bilaterally hip ultrasound and ultrasounds examinations also comes under 'Radiology'. This doesn't include any information or details regarding electrical activities in the human body which is monitored over a period of time. Bone Mineral Densitometry Exams reports, Bone Mineral Density reports or any other reports related to bone density also comes under 'Radiology' reports. This also includes documents generated from Diagnostic Image Center, Midtown Diagnostic Imaging. These documents might also contain scan quality, standard scores like the lumbar spine and hips etc. This documents will have the phrase 'This is based on Canadian Association of Radiologists Technical Standards for Reporting'. It is important to note that document is not a 'Radiology' document if it is requesting for a radiology report.

Photo: This refers to photographs related to the patient’s condition.

Consent: This refers to documents or forms that has patient consent which gives permission to specific healthcare providers to release patients medical records; this does not include the requests to transfer copy or original of the patients medical records to specific healthcare providers. The document will be titled 'Authorization to Release Medical Records'. These documents might also mention a 'Circle of Care' request, which means its a consent request. The form will be filled out by the patient (or their substitute decision maker) and includes their signature. 

Pathology: This refers to documents related to detail findings from tissue biopsy, fluid sample obtained from body fluids, PAP cytology, aspirates,  tissue scrapings, vaginal smear, yeast or bacterial vaginosis, cancerous and precancerous conditions, microbiology, for analysis and diagnosis. This category includes reports from a pathologist. These reports include reports on the presence or absence of yeast or bacterial vaginosis. This category does not include reports from a Dermatologist.

Diagnostics: This will be reports generated by electronic instruments that monitors patient's health over a period of time. This report also includes heart's electrical activity, providing valuable information on heart rate, rhythm abnormalities, symptoms such as palpitations, and potential correlations with daily activities or events which will aid a physician in diagnosing various cardiac conditions and guiding treatment decisions.

Pharmacy: This refers to documents sent from a pharmacist solely regarding medication renewals, as well as questions from a pharmacist at a pharmacy to a doctor. This does not include consultations from other physicians that also discuss medications as part of a consultation by a physician; these are consultations.

Requisition: The first or second page of these documents refer to requisition forms for patients to receive a diagnostic, imaging, or consultation. The first 1 to 2 pages must have patient demographic information including name, date of birth, health card number, address, phone number, ordering physician, and physician billing number, and fax number. The forms may have options to select from a list of various diagnostic tests, imaging modalities, body parts, medical specialties, and specialty departments. When the document contains more than two pages, the subsequent pages may contain attached medical records that come from a variety of sources and categories. Do not include the information on pages longer than 2 pages to infer the context of these documents. When the first 1 to 2 pages of these documents include a requisition form with patient demographic, ordering physician name, billing number, address, fax number, and also contain a selection of options for requesting diagnostic tests, imaging, and consultation; ignore the information on the subsequent pages after page 2.

HCC Referrals: This refers to documents for incoming referrals to family physicians at that clinic from health care connect.

Request: This refers to documents that requests for release for all medical records, reports and documents related to a patient's health history. This document may contain the phrases 'any and all information', ‘all medical records’, ‘complete file’, ‘cumulative patient profile’. These documents include requests to transfer medical records to another medical clinic, or family doctor.
